response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but their background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and product software (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang web services). The role demands SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. The closest relevant experience is low-latency backend work and brief C/C++ tooling development, which falls far short of principal-level hardware performance architecture.
strengths: Deep low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services handling millions of requests/sec)
15+ years of broad engineering experience, well above the years-of-experience requirement
Some systems-level C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components, audio DSP thesis work)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture experience
